segments.text: Call out now; Is there anyone who will answer you? And to which of the holy ones will you turn?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: For wrath kills a foolish man, And envy slays a simple one.
I have seen the foolish taking root, But suddenly I cursed his dwelling place.
His sons are far from safety, They are crushed in the gate, And there is no deliverer.
Because the hungry eat up his harvest, Taking it even from the thorns, And a snare snatches their substance.
For affliction does not come from the dust, Nor does trouble spring from the ground;
Yet man is born to trouble, As the sparks fly upward.
But as for me, I would seek God, And to God I would commit my cause—
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Who does great things, and unsearchable, Marvelous things without number.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: He gives rain on the earth, And sends waters on the fields.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He sets on high those who are lowly, And those who mourn are lifted to safety.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He frustrates the devices of the crafty, So that their hands cannot carry out their plans.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He catches the wise in their own craftiness, And the counsel of the cunning comes quickly upon them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: They meet with darkness in the daytime, And grope at noontime as in the night.
But He saves the needy from the sword, From the mouth of the mighty, And from their hand.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So the poor have hope, And injustice shuts her mouth.
Behold, happy is the man whom God corrects; Therefore do not despise the chastening of the Almighty.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: For He bruises, but He binds up; He wounds, but His hands make whole.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He shall deliver you in six troubles, Yes, in seven no evil shall touch you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: In famine He shall redeem you from death, And in war from the power of the sword.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall be hidden from the scourge of the tongue, And you shall not be afraid of destruction when it comes.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall laugh at destruction and famine, And you shall not be afraid of the beasts of the earth.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For you shall have a covenant with the stones of the field, And the beasts of the field shall be at peace with you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall know that your tent is in peace; You shall visit your dwelling and find nothing amiss.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall also know that your descendants shall be many, And your offspring like the grass of the earth.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall come to the grave at a full age, As a sheaf of grain ripens in its season.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Behold, this we have searched out; It is true. Hear it, and know for yourself.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz
